scenario: Catalyst Bank reads nested JSON for 21000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL output mapping must read the field address.city from the variable customer (a context). When customer is null, the expression must return the string "UNKNOWN" instead of failing the evaluation with a null error.
question: Which FEEL expression safely reads a nested context field with a default when the parent is null?

options.text: Use if customer != null then customer.address.city else "UNKNOWN" as a FEEL conditional that branches on the null check before path access
Use customer?.address?.city default "UNKNOWN" which is FEEL's optional-chaining operator that returns the default when any intermediate value is null
Use coalesce(customer.address.city, "UNKNOWN") which is a FEEL built-in helper that returns the second argument when the first evaluates to null
Use try (customer.address.city) catch "UNKNOWN" which is the FEEL exception-style fallback for null path traversal failures on context values

a.text: Correct. FEEL supports conditional expressions; the idiomatic null-safe form is an explicit if-then-else check before the path access.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL has no optional-chaining operator (?.); explicit conditionals or path-navigation are required.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL has no built-in coalesce function with that signature; the conditional form is documented.

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL has no try/catch syntax; the conditional form is documented.

explanation: FEEL has no optional-chaining or coalesce helper; the idiomatic null-safe pattern is an explicit if-then-else branch before path access.
